name: zabbix
label: Zabbix
description: Zabbix is an open-source, enterprise-grade monitoring platform that provides real-time visibility into the performance and availability of IT infrastructure, including servers, networks, applications, services, and cloud environments.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.name: server_url
fields.description: Provide the host URL of the zabbix server to connect and perform automated operations.
fields.tooltip: Provide the host URL of the zabbix server to connect and perform automated operations.

fields.title: Authentication Type
fields.name: auth_type
fields.description: Select the authentication mechanism supported by the Zabbix Server REST API. You can choose either 'Basic Auth' or 'Auth Token' Note: Basic Authentication is only supported in versions earlier than 7.0, while API Key authentication is supported in version 7.2 and later.
fields.tooltip: Select the authentication mechanism supported by the Zabbix Server REST API. You can choose either 'Basic Auth' or 'Auth Token' Note: Basic Authentication is only supported in versions earlier than 7.0, while API Key authentication is supported in version 7.2 and later.

Basic Auth.title: Authentication Method
Basic Auth.name: auth_method
Basic Auth.description: Select the authentication method to access the Zabbix REST API endpoints. You can choose either Client Credentials or an Auth Token.
Basic Auth.tooltip: Select the authentication method to access the Zabbix REST API endpoints. You can choose either Client Credentials or an Auth Token.

Client Credentials.title: Username
Client Credentials.name: username
Client Credentials.description: Specify the username used to access the Zabbix server.
Client Credentials.tooltip: Specify the username used to access the Zabbix server.

Client Credentials.title: Password
Client Credentials.name: password
Client Credentials.description: Specify the password used to access the Zabbix server.
Client Credentials.tooltip: Specify the password used to access the Zabbix server.

Auth Token.title: Auth Token
Auth Token.name: auth_token
Auth Token.description: Specify the authentication token used to access the Zabbix server.
Auth Token.tooltip: Specify the authentication token used to access the Zabbix server.

API Key.title: API Key
API Key.name: api_key
API Key.description: Specify the API key used for authentication and access to the Zabbix server.
API Key.tooltip: Specify the API key used for authentication and access to the Zabbix server.

fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.description: Specifies whether to verify the server's SSL certificate. By default, this option is enabled, i.e., set to true.
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for for the server is to be verified or not. By default, this option is set as True.

operations.title: Get Alerts
operations.description: Retrieves alert records from the Zabbix monitoring system, including details such as recipient, media type, status, and message content, based on specified input parameters.

parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Select the date and time after which alerts generated will be returned.
parameters.tooltip: Select the date and time after which alerts generated will be returned.

parameters.title: End Date
parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Select the date and time before which alerts generated will be returned.
parameters.tooltip: Select the date and time before which alerts generated will be returned.

parameters.title: Alert IDs
parameters.name: alert_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of alert IDs to return only the alerts with the specified IDs.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of alert IDs to return only the alerts with the specified IDs.

parameters.title: Action IDs
parameters.name: action_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of action IDs to return only the alerts generated by the specified actions.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of action IDs to return only the alerts generated by the specified actions.

parameters.title: Event IDs
parameters.name: event_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of event IDs to return only the alerts generated by the specified events.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of event IDs to return only the alerts generated by the specified events.

parameters.title: Group IDs
parameters.name: group_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of group IDs to return only the alerts generated by objects from the given host groups.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of group IDs to return only the alerts generated by objects from the given host groups.

parameters.title: Host IDs
parameters.name: host_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of host IDs to return only the alerts generated by objects from the given hosts.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of host IDs to return only the alerts generated by objects from the given hosts.

parameters.title: User IDs
parameters.name: user_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of user IDs to return only the alerts that were sent to the given users.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of user IDs to return only the alerts that were sent to the given users.

parameters.title: Object IDs
parameters.name: object_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of object IDs to return only the alerts generated by the given objects.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of object IDs to return only the alerts generated by the given objects.

parameters.title: Event Object Type
parameters.name: event_object_type
parameters.description: Select the event object types to return only the alerts generated by events related to objects of the given type. You can select from the following options: Trigger, Discovered Host, Discovered Service, Auto-Registered Host, Item, LLD Rule, Service.
parameters.tooltip: Select the event object types to return only the alerts generated by events related to objects of the given type. You can select from the following options: Trigger, Discovered Host, Discovered Service, Auto-Registered Host, Item, LLD Rule, Service.

parameters.title: Event Source
parameters.name: event_source
parameters.description: Select the event source to return only the alerts generated by events of the given source. You can select from the following options: Event Created By a Trigger, Event Created By a Discovered Rule, Event Created By Active Agent Auto Registration, Internal Event, Event Created on Service Status Upgrade.
parameters.tooltip: Select the event source to return only the alerts generated by events of the given source. You can select from the following options: Event Created By a Trigger, Event Created By a Discovered Rule, Event Created By Active Agent Auto Registration, Internal Event, Event Created on Service Status Upgrade.
options: Event Created by a Discovery Rule
Event Created by Active Agent Auto Registration
Event Created on Service Status Update

parameters.title: Sort Fields
parameters.name: sort_field
parameters.description: Select the properties on which to sort the results. You can select from the following options: alertid, clock, eventid, mediatypeid, sendto, status.
parameters.tooltip: Select the properties on which to sort the results. You can select from the following options: alertid, clock, eventid, mediatypeid, sendto, status.

parameters.title: Sort Order
parameters.name: sortorder
parameters.description: Select the order in which results are sorted. You can choose either ASC or DESC. If an array is passed, each value will be matched to the corresponding property given in the sortfield parameter.
parameters.tooltip: Select the order in which results are sorted. You can choose either ASC or DESC. If an array is passed, each value will be matched to the corresponding property given in the sortfield parameter.

parameters.title: Limit
parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Specify the maximum number of results this operation should return per page.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the maximum number of results this operation should return per page.

parameters.title: Search Pattern
parameters.name: search
parameters.description:
Specify a pattern (case-insensitive) to filter results. Specify a filter object in JSON format with key-value pairs, where the keys are property names and the values are strings to search in Zabbix. If no additional options are given, this will perform a LIKE "%â¦%" search. 
For example {"subject": "*CPU*","message": "*high*"}
parameters.tooltip:
Specify a pattern (case-insensitive) to filter results. Specify a filter object in JSON format with key-value pairs, where the keys are property names and the values are strings to search in Zabbix. If no additional options are given, this will perform a LIKE "%â¦%" search. 
For example {"subject": "*CPU*","message": "*high*"}

parameters.title: Search By Any
parameters.name: search_any
parameters.description: Select this option to return results that match any of the criterion given in the filter or search parameter instead of all of them. By default, this option is not selected, i.e., set to false.
parameters.tooltip: Select this option to return results that match any of the criterion given in the filter or search parameter instead of all of them. By default, this option is not selected, i.e., set to false.

parameters.title: Search Wildcards Enabled
parameters.name: search_wildcards_enabled
parameters.description: Select this option to enable the use of "*" as a wildcard character in the search parameter.
parameters.tooltip: Select this option to enable the use of "*" as a wildcard character in the search parameter.

parameters.title: Filter
parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Specifies whether to perform an exact match on the provided filter. Specify a filter object in JSON format with key-value pairs, where the keys are property names and the values are either a single value or an array of values to match in Zabbix. 
For example {"status": 0 }
parameters.tooltip:
Specifies whether to perform an exact match on the provided filter. Specify a filter object in JSON format with key-value pairs, where the keys are property names and the values are either a single value or an array of values to match in Zabbix. 
For example {"status": 0 }

parameters.title: Additional Fields
parameters.name: additional_fields
parameters.description: Specify additional parameters in JSON format to filter the alerts based on the provided input.
parameters.tooltip: Specify additional parameters in JSON format to filter the alerts based on the provided input.

operations.title: Get Events
operations.description: Retrieves event data from the Zabbix server using filters such as event type, severity, source, and time range.

parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Select the date and time after which events generated will be returned.
parameters.tooltip: Select the date and time after which events generated will be returned.

parameters.title: End Date
parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Select the date and time after which events created will be returned.
parameters.tooltip: Select the date and time after which events created will be returned.

parameters.title: Event IDs
parameters.name: event_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of event IDs to return only the events with the specified IDs.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of event IDs to return only the events with the specified IDs.

parameters.title: Group IDs
parameters.name: group_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of host group IDs to return only the events with the specified IDs.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of host group IDs to return only the events with the specified IDs.

parameters.title: Host IDs
parameters.name: host_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of host IDs to return only events created by objects that belong to the given hosts.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of host IDs to return only events created by objects that belong to the given hosts.

parameters.title: Object IDs
parameters.name: object_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of object IDs to return only events created by the given objects.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of object IDs to return only events created by the given objects.

parameters.title: Event Object Type
parameters.name: event_object_type
parameters.description: Select the event object types to return only the generated by objects of the given type. You can select from the following options: Trigger, Discovered Host, Discovered Service, Auto-Registered Host, Item, LLD Rule, Service
parameters.tooltip: Select the event object types to return only the generated by objects of the given type. You can select from the following options: Trigger, Discovered Host, Discovered Service, Auto-Registered Host, Item, LLD Rule, Service

parameters.title: Event Source
parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Select the event source to return only the events generated by the given source. You can select from the following options: Event Created By a Trigger, Event Created By a Discovered Rule, Event Created By Active Agent Auto Registration, Internal Event, Event Created on Service Status Upgrade.
parameters.tooltip: Select the event source to return only the events generated by the given source. You can select from the following options: Event Created By a Trigger, Event Created By a Discovered Rule, Event Created By Active Agent Auto Registration, Internal Event, Event Created on Service Status Upgrade.
options: Event Created by a Discovery Rule
Event Created by Active Agent Auto Registration

parameters.title: Acknowledged
parameters.name: acknowledged
parameters.description: Select this option to return only acknowledged events. By default, this option is not selected, i.e., this operation returns unacknowledged events.
parameters.tooltip: Select this option to return only acknowledged events. By default, this option is not selected, i.e., this operation returns unacknowledged events.

parameters.title: Suppressed
parameters.name: suppressed
parameters.description: Select this option to return only suppressed events. By default, this option is not selected, i.e., this operation returns events in the normal state.
parameters.tooltip: Select this option to return only suppressed events. By default, this option is not selected, i.e., this operation returns events in the normal state.

parameters.title: Severities
parameters.name: severities
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of event severities to return only the events with the specified severity.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of event severities to return only the events with the specified severity.

parameters.title: Start Event ID
parameters.name: event_id_from
parameters.description: Specify an event ID to return only events with IDs greater or equal to the given ID.
parameters.tooltip: Specify an event ID to return only events with IDs greater or equal to the given ID.

parameters.title: End Event ID
parameters.name: event_id_till
parameters.description: Specify an event ID to return only events with IDs lesser or equal to the given ID.
parameters.tooltip: Specify an event ID to return only events with IDs lesser or equal to the given ID.

parameters.title: Sort Fields
parameters.name: sort_field
parameters.description: Select the properties on which to sort the results. You can select from the following options: eventid, objectid, clock.
parameters.tooltip: Select the properties on which to sort the results. You can select from the following options: eventid, objectid, clock.

parameters.title: Sort Order
parameters.name: sortorder
parameters.description:
Select the order in which results are sorted. You can choose either ASC or DESC. 
If an array is passed, each value will be matched to the corresponding property given in the sortfield parameter.
parameters.tooltip:
Select the order in which results are sorted. You can choose either ASC or DESC. 
If an array is passed, each value will be matched to the corresponding property given in the sortfield parameter.

parameters.title: Limit
parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Specify the maximum number of results this operation should return per page.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the maximum number of results this operation should return per page.

parameters.title: Search Pattern
parameters.name: search
parameters.description:
Specify a pattern (case-insensitive) to filter results. Specify a filter object in JSON format with key-value pairs, where the keys are property names and the values are strings to search in Zabbix. If no additional options are given, this will perform a LIKE "%â¦%" search. 
For example, {"name": "CPU"}
parameters.tooltip:
Specify a pattern (case-insensitive) to filter results. Specify a filter object in JSON format with key-value pairs, where the keys are property names and the values are strings to search in Zabbix. If no additional options are given, this will perform a LIKE "%â¦%" search. 
For example, {"name": "CPU"}

parameters.title: Filter
parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Specifies whether to perform an exact match on the provided filter. Specify a filter object in JSON format with key-value pairs, where the keys are property names and the values are either a single value or an array of values to match in Zabbix. 
For example,{"value": 1,"acknowledged": 0}.
parameters.tooltip:
Specifies whether to perform an exact match on the provided filter. Specify a filter object in JSON format with key-value pairs, where the keys are property names and the values are either a single value or an array of values to match in Zabbix. 
For example,{"value": 1,"acknowledged": 0}.

parameters.title: Additional Fields
parameters.name: additional_fields
parameters.description: Specify additional parameters in JSON format to filter the alerts based on the provided input.
parameters.tooltip: Specify additional parameters in JSON format to filter the alerts based on the provided input.

operations.title: Get Problems
operations.description: Retrieves active problem records from the Zabbix server, representing currently unresolved issues, based on specified input parameters.

parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Select the date and time at or after which problems created will be returned.
parameters.tooltip: Select the date and time at or after which problems created will be returned.

parameters.title: End Date
parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Select the date and time at or before which problems created will be returned.
parameters.tooltip: Select the date and time at or before which problems created will be returned.

parameters.title: Fetch Related Alerts
parameters.name: fetch_alert
parameters.description: Select this option to retrieve problem-related alerts from the Zabbix server.
parameters.tooltip: Select this option to retrieve problem-related alerts from the Zabbix server.

parameters.title: Severities
parameters.name: severities
parameters.description: Select the severities options to return only the problems with the specified severity. You can select from the following options: Not classified, Information, Warning, Average, High, Disaster.
parameters.tooltip: Select the severities options to return only the problems with the specified severity. You can select from the following options: Not classified, Information, Warning, Average, High, Disaster.

parameters.title: Event IDs
parameters.name: event_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of event IDs to return only the problems created by the given events.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of event IDs to return only the problems created by the given events.

parameters.title: Group IDs
parameters.name: group_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of host group IDs to return only the problems created by the given host groups.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of host group IDs to return only the problems created by the given host groups.

parameters.title: Host IDs
parameters.name: host_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of host IDs to return only problems created by objects that belong to the given hosts.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of host IDs to return only problems created by objects that belong to the given hosts.

parameters.title: Object IDs
parameters.name: object_ids
parameters.description: Specify a CSV list of object IDs to return only problems created by the given objects.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a CSV list of object IDs to return only problems created by the given objects.

parameters.title: Event Object Type
parameters.name: object
parameters.description: Select the event object types to return only problems generated by event objects of the given type. You can select from the following options: Trigger, Discovered Host, Discovered Service, Auto-Registered Host, Item, LLD Rule, Service.
parameters.tooltip: Select the event object types to return only problems generated by event objects of the given type. You can select from the following options: Trigger, Discovered Host, Discovered Service, Auto-Registered Host, Item, LLD Rule, Service.

parameters.title: Event Source
parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Select the event source to return only the problems generated by the given event source. You can select from the following options: Event Created By a Trigger, Event Created By a Discovered Rule, Event Created By Active Agent Auto Registration, Internal Event, Event Created on Service Status Upgrade.
parameters.tooltip: Select the event source to return only the problems generated by the given event source. You can select from the following options: Event Created By a Trigger, Event Created By a Discovered Rule, Event Created By Active Agent Auto Registration, Internal Event, Event Created on Service Status Upgrade.

operations.title: Execute Generic JSON RPC Request
operations.description: Executes any Zabbix JSON-RPC call using the provided request body parameters.

parameters.title: Request Body
parameters.description:
Specify the JSON-RPC request body to execute and retrieve the response from Zabbix. 
Note: Do not include the auth key and value in the request body, as it is internally handled by the code. For more information, see the https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/5.0/en/manual/api/reference/host/get link
parameters.tooltip:
Specify the JSON-RPC request body to execute and retrieve the response from Zabbix. 
Note: Do not include the auth key and value in the request body, as it is internally handled by the code. For more information, see the https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/5.0/en/manual/api/reference/host/get link
